context.projectOverview: agent-bober TypeScript/ESM CLI. Plan spec-20260605-scale-safe-history-memory Layer 2 begins here. Sprint 2 adds a deterministic, append-only lessons store under .bober/memory/ (MEMORY.md-style index + one md file per lesson, mandatory non-empty sourceEntryRefs provenance). Schema + persistence + bounded read ONLY — no distillation, no CLI, no planner wiring, no history reads.

completedSprints.title: Bounded history reads + crash-safe rotation
completedSprints.summary: Added loadRecentHistory tail reader, crash-safe rotation to history.archive.jsonl gated on new history.maxActiveLines zod field (default 2000); loadHistory now reads archive+active concatenated, signatures unchanged. Lives in src/state/history.ts + src/state/history-rotation.ts. PASSED iter 1 (commits a75a376, 59dde7d).

contract.title: Deterministic lessons memory store (LessonEntry schema + .bober/memory state module)
contract.description: Add a new append-only lessons store under .bober/memory/, mirroring the MEMORY.md curated-index pattern. Introduce a zod LessonEntry schema whose sourceEntryRefs provenance array is mandatory and non-empty, persistence that writes one markdown file per lesson plus a single bounded INDEX.md line, and a capped index reader that never opens individual lesson files. This sprint is schema + persistence + bounded read only — no distillation logic, no CLI, no planner wiring.

successCriteria.description: LessonEntrySchema in src/state/memory.ts is a zod object with lessonId, createdAt, category, tags (string[]), summary, occurrences (positive int), severity (enum), and sourceEntryRefs (non-empty string[]); a test asserts a valid lesson parses and a lesson with an empty sourceEntryRefs array is rejected.

appendLesson writes .bober/memory/<lessonId>.md (front-matter plus body) and upserts a single one-line entry into .bober/memory/INDEX.md; a test asserts both files exist and INDEX.md gains exactly one line per distinct lessonId across repeated appends of the same id.

loadLessonIndex(projectRoot, { limit }) returns at most `limit` index records parsed from INDEX.md without opening any individual lesson file; a test asserts the cap and that no <lessonId>.md read is issued during the index load.

loadLesson(projectRoot, lessonId) round-trips a persisted LessonEntry, and the provenance invariant holds: every persisted lesson carries at least one sourceEntryRef; a test writes then reads a lesson and asserts equality plus a non-empty sourceEntryRefs.
npm run typecheck and npm run build exit 0 and the new src/state/memory tests pass with no new failures beyond the documented flaky baseline.

nonGoals: Do not compute or distill lessons in this sprint — only the schema, persistence, and bounded index read.
Do not call any LLM, embedding, or network API.
Do not wire the planner or any CLI command to this store yet.
Do not read or modify history.jsonl in this sprint.
stopConditions: Stop when LessonEntry, appendLesson, loadLessonIndex, and loadLesson exist with passing round-trip and provenance tests and a bounded index read.
Stop when changes are confined to src/state/memory.ts and its collocated test.
contract.definitionOfDone: A deterministic, append-only lessons store exists under .bober/memory/: each lesson persists as <lessonId>.md with structured front-matter and a one-line entry in a bounded INDEX.md, mirroring the MEMORY.md pattern. The zod LessonEntry schema enforces a non-empty sourceEntryRefs provenance array, loadLessonIndex offers a capped read that never opens individual lesson files, and typecheck, build, and tests pass.
assumptions: No .bober/memory directory exists today (verified via ls), so the store is greenfield and additive.
Mirroring src/state/history.ts (path helpers plus zod safeParse plus append) keeps the module consistent with existing state code.
contract.generatorNotes: Create src/state/memory.ts mirroring history.ts conventions. Define memoryDir(projectRoot) = .bober/memory, lessonPath(root, id) = .bober/memory/<id>.md, indexPath(root) = .bober/memory/INDEX.md. LessonEntrySchema = z.object({ lessonId: z.string().min(1), createdAt: z.string().datetime(), category: z.string().min(1), tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]), summary: z.string().min(1), occurrences: z.number().int().positive(), severity: z.enum(['info','warn','high']), sourceEntryRefs: z.array(z.string().min(1)).min(1) }). appendLesson: ensureDir, write <id>.md as YAML-ish front-matter (between --- fences) carrying the structured fields plus a human-readable body, then upsert one INDEX.md line of the form `- <lessonId> [<category>/<severity>] (x<occurrences>) tags: a,b — <summary first 80 chars>`; upsert means replace the existing line for that lessonId or append if absent (read INDEX.md, split lines, filter out the prior line for this id, push the new line, rewrite). loadLessonIndex parses INDEX.md lines into lightweight records { lessonId, category, severity, occurrences, tags, summarySnippet } and returns the last `limit`. loadLesson reads <id>.md, parses the front-matter, and validates via LessonEntrySchema.safeParse. Use import type; ESM .js specifiers; box-drawing headers. Conventional commit: bober(sprint-2): deterministic lessons memory store.
contract.evaluatorNotes: Verify LessonEntrySchema rejects empty sourceEntryRefs and accepts a valid lesson. Verify appendLesson creates <id>.md and a single INDEX.md line, and that appending the same lessonId twice keeps INDEX.md at one line for that id (upsert, not duplicate). Verify loadLessonIndex caps to `limit` and does not open per-lesson files (assert via spy or by deleting a <id>.md and confirming the index load still succeeds). Verify loadLesson round-trips a written lesson and the provenance invariant. Run npm run typecheck (exit 0), npm run build (exit 0), and the memory tests. Confirm git diff is confined to src/state/memory.ts and its test.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
